articles.title: HOW STARTUPS CAN USE CX TO DRIVE BREAK-OUT GROWTH
VENTURE CAPITAL IS NOT COMPETITIVE
articles.summary: Venture capital no longer behaves like a competitive market. The language of rivalry persists, but the substance has thinned. The same firms crowd around the same deals, rehearsing the same convictions until they harden into consensus. Somewhere along the way, the infinite game became finite. What began as a search for outliers has become an exercise in pattern recognition. Capital pools, performance converges, and the illusion of competition masks a quiet retreat from risk. The question is not who wins this round, but whether there's still a game being played at all.

articles.title: THE $100B QUESTION: HOW SAAS GIANTS ARE REWRITING THE RULES OF VALUE WITH AI IN 2025
articles.summary: SaaS pricing ran on predictability for two decades. Seats and flat plans made sense when value scaled with people. AI broke that logic - one agent can do the work of ten, and each query can carry a volatile compute bill. Companies are now rebuilding their models in real time. Hybrid pricing buys time but not clarity. Outcome pricing promises fairness but demands proof. Between the two lies a growing realization that software was predictable only because the people using it were.

articles.title: SAM ALTMAN ON OPENAI'S REVENUE
articles.summary: OpenAI's annual revenue run rate has surpassed $20B. Future infrastructure commitments are reaching $1.4 trillion. The company's plans include enterprise offerings, AI cloud services, and ventures into consumer devices and scientific discovery.

articles.title: HOW YOU.COM SURVIVED CHATGPT WITH A PIVOT TO ENTERPRISE AI
articles.summary: You.com shifted from consumer search to enterprise AI, offering search APIs and complex data analytics to businesses following ChatGPT's launch. Competing with Google and others in the AI market, You.com leverages its own search infrastructure to deliver detailed reports and agent-based solutions like business analytics and financial analysis. The company also introduced Auto Mode for integrating multiple LLMs and secured $100M in VC funding to expand its agent-centric services.

articles.title: WHY OPENAI WANTS A PIECE OF AMD
articles.summary: OpenAI will buy up to 6 GW of AMD GPUs, starting with 1 GW of MI450 in H2 2026, and get $0.01 warrants for up to 160M shares that vest with 1–6 GW deployments and other milestones, potentially reaching ~10% ownership. It chose AMD because Nvidia supply is tight and pricey, AMD claims MI450 will beat current Nvidia parts, and AMD's smaller base trades equity for multi‑GW orders. Monetization trails GPU demand, especially in video, and tariff‑driven inflation cuts rate‑cut odds, raising downside risk for AI‑levered names.

articles.title: LOOK FOR YES
articles.summary: Product and legal teams often clash due to misunderstood risk levels. Risk managers should ensure their advice isn't seen as an absolute veto but rather as guidance by framing context and trade-offs. Product managers need to actively seek solutions to legal concerns, aiming for responsible risk-taking rather than defaulting to "no."

articles.title: BREAKING: 🥊 THE GREAT STARTUP DEBATE: 996 VS. THE 4-DAY WORK WEEK
articles.summary: Some founders swear by 12-HOUR DAYS and 6-day weeks. Others claim the 4-day work week builds better teams _and_ products. _Who's right?_

articles.title: AGNES AI
articles.summary: Agnes AI is a collaborative AI agent that lets teams co-edit, share memory, and build slides, reports, and strategy docs together in real time.

articles.title: KOAH LAUNCHES: ADSENSE FOR GENAI
articles.summary: Koah introduces AdSense-like monetization for GenAI apps by integrating context-aware ads into chat. With proven success in apps like Liner and Luzia, developers see high revenue without compromising user experience, averaging $10 ECPM and 3-5x higher engagement rates.

articles.title: THE LOST ART OF CRYING
articles.summary: Former Facebook VP Julie Zhuo just wrote the most vulnerable thing you'll read about founder psychology this year. She reveals how Silicon Valley's "ice queen" culture (the obsession with logic, optimization, and emotional control) is actually a fear response, not a superpower. As AI takes over rational work like calculations and planning, the only irreplaceable human skill is our ability to truly feel: grief, awe, connection, vulnerability. Zhuo discovered this through what she calls "Big Cries" using music and solitude, and argues that in an era where we're building superhuman machines, learning to cry again isn't weakness but reclaiming the last thing AI can never touch.

articles.title: THE INTERNET WANTS TO BE FRAGMENTED
articles.summary: The social media empire is crumbling, and Gen Z is leading the exodus. Nearly half of Gen Z plans to spend less time on social networks in 2025, abandoning the "town square" platforms for intimate Discord channels, DMs, and curated feeds. The big experiment of throwing humanity into one digital room failed. This shift back to small, self-selected communities is a massive opportunity for builders creating tools for micro-communities instead of trying to be the next Twitter.

articles.title: SUBTLE COMPUTING'S VOICE ISOLATION MODELS HELP COMPUTERS UNDERSTAND YOU IN NOISY ENVIRONMENTS
articles.summary: Subtle Computing's voice-isolation models enhance voice recognition in noisy environments, benefiting AI products and services.
